themes.title: Rising voluntary benefit adoption among SMBs
themes.summary: Small and medium businesses are rapidly expanding their voluntary benefits offerings, driven by competitive hiring pressures and employee demand for more personalized coverage. Employers with 10–500 employees now represent the fastest-growing segment for voluntary benefit enrollment.

data_points.text: 62% of SMBs added at least one voluntary benefit in the past 18 months
Voluntary benefit enrollment grew 28% year-over-year in companies with under 200 employees
Top voluntary benefits requested by employees: dental (74%), vision (71%), life (68%), accident (51%)

themes.title: Prevention-first benefits reduce claims and improve retention
themes.summary: Companies that integrate wellness and prevention programs into their benefits stack are seeing measurable improvements in claims outcomes and employee retention. The link between proactive health engagement and reduced healthcare costs is now well-documented across SMB populations.

data_points.text: Employers with prevention-first programs report 19% lower claims costs over 3 years
Employees who engage with wellness platforms are 2.4x more likely to renew voluntary benefits
Prevention-linked benefit enrollment conversion is 31% higher than traditional enrollment methods

themes.title: Benefits confusion is the top reason employees leave coverage on the table
themes.summary: Despite expanded benefits offerings, utilization rates remain low primarily due to complexity and poor communication. Employees report not understanding their options as the single biggest barrier to enrollment in voluntary benefits.

data_points.text: 58% of employees say they don't understand their voluntary benefit options well enough to enroll
data_points.source_name: MetLife U.S. Employee Benefit Trends Study 2025

data_points.text: Only 34% of eligible employees actively use more than one voluntary benefit
data_points.source_name: MetLife U.S. Employee Benefit Trends Study 2025

data_points.text: Companies with digital-first enrollment tools see 2x enrollment rates vs. paper-based processes
data_points.source_name: MetLife U.S. Employee Benefit Trends Study 2025

themes.source: MetLife U.S. Employee Benefit Trends Study 2025

themes.title: Brokers are differentiating on benefits experience, not just price
themes.summary: The traditional price-led broker conversation is giving way to value-led conversations about employee experience, utilization rates, and retention outcomes. SMB brokers who can demonstrate measurable benefit engagement are winning and retaining more clients.

data_points.text: 71% of HR directors say they would switch brokers for one who could improve employee benefit utilization
Brokers who offer digital enrollment tools retain clients 40% longer on average
SMB employer NPS for brokers who offer wellness-integrated benefits is 34 points higher than traditional brokers

themes.title: Carriers expanding distribution through embedded benefit platforms
themes.summary: Traditional carrier distribution channels are being supplemented by embedded benefit platforms that integrate coverage options into daily wellness journeys. This shift is dramatically improving conversion rates and reducing acquisition costs for carriers targeting SMB populations.

data_points.text: Embedded distribution channels deliver 2.6x higher conversion rates than traditional broker-direct models
Carrier acquisition costs in embedded channels are 45% lower than through traditional intermediaries
71% of carriers plan to increase investment in embedded benefits distribution by 2027

themes.title: Gen Z and Millennial employees are reshaping benefits expectations
themes.summary: The two largest workforce cohorts now expect benefits that are digital-first, personalized, and tied to their health and lifestyle goals. Traditional one-size-fits-all benefits packages are increasingly seen as inadequate by younger employees.

data_points.text: 78% of Gen Z employees say personalized benefits would make them more loyal to their employer
Millennials and Gen Z now make up 67% of the SMB workforce
Benefits are the #2 reason employees cite for staying at a job, behind salary but ahead of career growth

themes.title: Public Concern Over Prescription Drug Affordability Reaches New High
themes.summary: A KFF Health Tracking Poll finds that a growing majority of the public is worried about affording prescription drugs. Large majorities across political parties support greater government regulation of drug prices, coinciding with the Trump administration's TrumpRx initiative.

data_points.text: A growing majority of the public worries about being able to afford prescription drugs, reaching a new high.
Large majorities across political parties want the government to do more to regulate prescription drug prices.

themes.title: Public Concern Over Prescription Drug Affordability Reaches New High
themes.summary: A new KFF Health Tracking Poll finds that a growing majority of the public is worried about affording prescription drugs. Large majorities across political parties support greater government regulation of drug prices, coinciding with the Trump administration's TrumpRx initiative.

data_points.text: A growing majority of the public worries about being able to afford prescription drugs, reaching a new high.
Large majorities across political parties want the government to do more to regulate prescription drug prices.
